agent_runs.outcome: validation_rejected (1 error: output_mapping no-output channel with empty fields)
restructured output_mapping to []; submitted (19 claims, 14 deps)
ACCEPT (33/33 coverage: 18 supported, 1 partially_supported medium, 14/14 deps confirmed)

notes: First-round ACCEPT: no gate REVERT, no BLOCKED. Cheapest same-model config after haiku-haiku, with far less churn.

The opus author was the only one to identify the custom persistence table ZABAPGIT as a dependency, and correctly excluded all zcl_abapgit_*/zif_abapgit_* names as self-references (defined in the same file).

The opus judge verified beyond the cited evidence (e.g. confirmed FORM password_popup attribution by reading the surrounding code) instead of blindly rejecting out-of-range wording - fewer false-positive REVERTs than the sonnet judge on comparable claims.
The author avoided fragile whole-file exact counts, so no count-claim disputes arose (the class that caused REVERTs in both sonnet configs and haiku-haiku).

agent_runs.outcome: submitted (11 gaps: 9 auto-answered, 2 open - correctly refused to auto-close owner-stated facts because owner input is not an auto-research source)
questionnaire (2 questions) -> owner's statement captured via capture-answer: 2 gaps answered (governance PURPOSE, scheduling TRIGGER)
submitted first try (15 FUN + 7 PRC claims), self-validated
ACCEPT (15/15 all supported, 1 at medium; zero contradictions)

notes: The opus researcher showed the sharpest source-discipline of the benchmark: given the owner's statement upfront, it still kept those 2 gaps open because owner input is not one of the four auto-research sources - it staged them for the formal capture-answer instead of laundering them into raw-docs.
Zero load-bearing gaps open at the end; every phase first-try; the only medium-confidence item was a tight-entailment judgment call.
Methodology note: the owner's deployment statement was provided in the researcher prompt for the opus and fable configs (it had emerged during the sonnet configs' questionnaire loop).
